title: 10 possible side effects of Ginkgo biloba (Ginkgo) if used improperly
intro: Ginkgo biloba, a popular herbal supplement, can have several potential side effects if used improperly. Firstly, it can increase the risk of bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties, which can be problematic during surgeries or for those with bleeding disorders. Secondly, it may interact with other medications, such as blood thinners and antidepressants, leading to adverse effects. Thirdly, ginkgo biloba can lower blood pressure, which, if already low, can cause dizziness, fainting, or falls. Additionally, it may cause stomach upset, headaches, and skin rashes in some individuals. Lastly, excessive consumption of ginkgo biloba during pregnancy and lactation is discouraged, as its safety and efficacy have not been established in these conditions.
